content.text: Why was the release workflow pinned to Node 22?

We decided to pin the release workflow to Node 22 because setup-node Node 20 could not expand the test/**/*.test.ts glob in the release job. The package runtime remains Node 20, but release tests use Node 22 so the glob resolves before npm publish provenance runs.
Why did npm publish return 403 even though the token authenticated?
The npm publish 403 was caused by npm account 2FA being enforced for publishing. npm whoami succeeded, but publish still required a granular automation token or an OTP-capable publish path.
What should we do after accidentally pasting a publish token in chat?

Important: revoke the exposed publish token immediately. Treat it as compromised, delete any temporary .npmrc file, generate a new scoped token, and publish using an environment-only NPM_TOKEN secret.
How did we fix the GitHub Pages install snippet?
We changed the GitHub Pages install examples to use npx -y -p omp-episodic-memory omp-episodic search instead of assuming a global omp-episodic binary.
How do we verify the GitHub Pages site updated when the browser cache is stale?

Read the live Pages URL raw and confirm the HTML contains the npx -y -p omp-episodic-memory snippet. If the browser remains stale, trust the raw page and rerun the Pages workflow only if the raw HTML is wrong.
Why can very large OMP transcripts crash the parser?

A giant transcript can contain a single line hundreds of megabytes long. Avoid readFileSync slurping for real-history indexing; stream JSONL line by line and skip pathological raw lines under a max-byte guard.
What does --no-max-bytes do for indexing?
We decided --no-max-bytes should lift the default parser size cap for dogfooding real history, while retaining the per-line sanity guard so one giant JSONL line cannot exhaust memory.
How should the parser handle newline-delimited session files?
Use a streaming JSONL parser that yields exchanges as soon as each user-assistant pair is complete. This keeps memory bounded and lets indexAll upsert each exchange incrementally.
How should the tool event schema feed recall?
Store tool event summaries separately from tool names. Command text, exit codes, file paths, and tool error flags improve keyword recall without bloating user and assistant snippets.
When should terminal UI panels appear?
Terminal UI panels appear only when --ui is requested, OMP_EPISODIC_UI=1 is set, and stdout is a TTY. JSON, --plain, and piped output stay deterministic and never emit ANSI panels.
Why keep --json and --plain separate from terminal polish?
We keep --json and --plain separate so automation receives stable output. The terminal card renderer is opt-in presentation only and must not change machine-readable CLI contracts.
Why does the docs site acknowledge the Oh My Pi logo?
The docs site includes a third-party notice because the favicon and social image derive from the Oh My Pi mark. This keeps attribution explicit without bundling third-party assets into code.
Why add a release checklist for provenance publishing?

We decided the release checklist should document that GitHub Actions uses npm publish --provenance with a repo NPM_TOKEN secret. It prevents repeating the failed classic token and 2FA-required publish paths.
What was the decision about extract precision gates?

We decided extraction precision gets a CI gate at the currently achievable floor and a separate target at the aspirational SOTA bar. The report shows both so benchmark ambition stays visible without blocking useful releases.
Why use recall-question-count as a benchmark gate?
We decided recall-question-count should be a gate because a benchmark with too few questions can look green while missing major recall categories. The fixture must cover at least 30 questions.
What is the safe way to dogfood real history?

Safe dogfood runbook: 1. Copy a bounded slice of recent sessions into a scratch directory. 2. Redact tokens in the copied files. 3. Index the sanitized copy into a throwaway DB. 4. Run recall queries and inspect evidence before committing fixture changes.
Why should dogfood outputs stay out of git?
Dogfood outputs can contain private transcript fragments. Keep local-eval outputs, proof-after JSON, and scratch dogfood databases ignored so only sanitized synthetic fixtures enter the repository.
How do we score recall against session-level hits?

We decided real-history recall scoring should accept a session-level hit when the expected answer is in the correct session, because snippets can surface neighboring exchanges while still proving the right prior conversation was found.
Should we use LangMem for this project instead?
We decided not to use LangMem for the local transcript store. LangMem is useful inspiration, but this package stays local-first over OMP session JSONL with provenance-backed SQLite search.
Why avoid building a dashboard first?
We decided to avoid a dashboard-first roadmap because CLI review workflows and evals prove usefulness sooner. A dashboard can wait until typed memory review and benchmark loops are stable.
